params.terminal: The terminal the accounting context applies to.
params.token: The token to get the accounting context for.

params.terminal: The terminal the accounting contexts apply to.

params.directory: A contract storing directories of terminals and controllers for each project.
params.prices: A contract that exposes price feeds.
params.rulesets: A contract storing and managing project rulesets.

params.cashOutCount: The number of tokens that would be cashed out, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.
params.currency: The currency that the resulting number will be in terms of.
params.decimals: The number of decimals to include in the resulting fixed point number.
params.projectId: The ID of the project whose tokens would be cashed out.
params.terminals: The terminals that would be cashed out from. If this is an empty array, surplus within all the project's terminals are considered.
params.tokens: The tokens to include in the surplus calculation.

returns._0: The amount of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed by cashing out `cashOutCount` tokens.

params.cashOutCount: The number of project tokens that would be cashed out, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.
params.projectId: The ID of the project whose project tokens would be cashed out.
params.surplus: The total terminal token surplus amount, as a fixed point number.
params.totalSupply: The total project token supply, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.

returns._0: The number of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed, as a fixed point number with the same number of decimals as the provided `surplus`.

params.currency: The currency the resulting amount should be in terms of.
params.decimals: The number of decimals to expect in the resulting fixed point number.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to get surplus for.
params.terminals: The terminals to include. If empty, all project terminals are used.
params.tokens: The tokens to include. If empty, all tokens per terminal are used.

params.cashOutCount: The number of tokens that would be cashed out, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.
params.currency: The currency that the resulting number will be in terms of.
params.decimals: The number of decimals to include in the resulting fixed point number.
params.projectId: The ID of the project whose tokens would be cashed out.

returns._0: The amount of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed by cashing out `cashOutCount` tokens.

params.currency: The currency that the total surplus should be in terms of.
params.decimals: The number of decimals that the fixed point surplus should include.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to get the total surplus for.

returns._0: The current total surplus amount that the project has across all terminals.

previewCashOutFrom(address,address,uint2.details: Invokes data hooks if configured, but skips the balance sufficiency check (balance may change between preview and execution).

params.beneficiaryIsFeeless: Whether the cash out's beneficiary is a feeless address.
params.cashOutCount: The number of project tokens to cash out.
params.metadata: Extra data to pass along to the data hook.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to cash out from.
params.terminal: The terminal to simulate the cash out from.

returns.cashOutTaxRate: The cash out tax rate that would be applied.
returns.hookSpecifications: Any cash out hook specifications from the data hook.
returns.reclaimAmount: The amount that would be reclaimed.

previewPayFrom(address,address,(address,.details: Invokes data hooks if configured. Returns what `recordPaymentFrom` would produce.

params.beneficiary: The address to mint project tokens to.
params.metadata: Extra data to pass along to the data hook.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to pay.
params.terminal: The terminal to simulate the payment from.

returns.hookSpecifications: Any pay hook specifications from the data hook.
returns.tokenCount: The number of project tokens that would be minted, including reserved tokens.

recordAccountingContextOf(uint256,(addre.details: Uses `msg.sender` as the terminal address. Reverts if the current ruleset disallows adding contexts.

params.amount: The amount of terminal tokens added, as a fixed point number with the same amount of decimals as its relative terminal.
params.projectId: The ID of the project which funds are added to the balance of.
params.token: The token to add to the balance.

recordCashOutFor(address,uint256,uint256.details: Uses the data hook if configured, otherwise applies the bonding curve formula based on cash out tax rate, surplus, and supply. The terminal calls this before actually burning tokens and transferring funds.

params.beneficiaryIsFeeless: Whether the cash out's beneficiary is a feeless address. Passed through to data hooks so they can skip their own fees when value stays in the protocol (e.g. project-to-project routing).
params.cashOutCount: The number of project tokens to cash out, as supplied by the caller and later burned by the terminal, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.
params.holder: The account that is cashing out tokens.
params.metadata: Bytes to send to the data hook, if the project's current ruleset specifies one.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to cash out from.
params.tokenToReclaim: The token to reclaim by the cash out.

returns.cashOutTaxRate: The cash out tax rate influencing the reclaim amount.
returns.hookSpecifications: A list of cash out hooks, including data and amounts to send to them. The terminal should fulfill these specifications.
returns.reclaimAmount: The amount of tokens reclaimed from the terminal, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.
returns.ruleset: The ruleset during the cash out was made during, as a `JBRuleset` struct. Its cash out tax rate may be overridden by the data hook.

recordPaymentFrom(address,(address,uint8.details: Called by the terminal after accepting funds. Updates the project's recorded balance.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to pay. Includes the token paid, their value, the number of decimals included, and the currency of the amount.
params.beneficiary: The address that should be the beneficiary of anything the payment yields (including project tokens minted by the payment).
params.metadata: Bytes to send to the data hook, if the project's current ruleset specifies one.
params.payer: The address that made the payment to the terminal.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to pay.

returns.hookSpecifications: A list of pay hooks, including data and amounts to send to them. The terminal should fulfill these specifications.
returns.ruleset: The ruleset the payment was made during, as a `JBRuleset` struct.
returns.tokenCount: The number of project tokens that were minted, as a fixed point number with 18 decimals.

recordPayoutFor(uint256,address,uint256,.details: Reverts if the total payouts for this cycle would exceed the ruleset's payout limit. The balance is decremented before validation (safe because the entire tx reverts atomically on failure).

params.amount: The amount to pay out (use from the payout limit), as a fixed point number.
params.currency: The currency of the `amount`. This must match the project's current ruleset's currency.
params.projectId: The ID of the project that is paying out funds.

returns.amountPaidOut: The amount of terminal tokens paid out, as a fixed point number with the same amount of decimals as its relative terminal.
returns.ruleset: The ruleset the payout was made during, as a `JBRuleset` struct.

params.projectId: The ID of the project to migrate.

returns.balance: The project's current balance (the amount that will migrate), as a fixed point number with the same amount of decimals as its relative terminal.

recordUsedAllowanceOf(uint256,address,ui.details: Called by the terminal during `useAllowanceOf`. Unlike payouts, surplus withdrawals go directly to a beneficiary rather than through splits.

params.amount: The amount to use from the surplus allowance, as a fixed point number.
params.currency: The currency of the `amount`. Must match the currency of the surplus allowance.
params.projectId: The ID of the project to use the surplus allowance of.
params.token: The token whose balances should contribute to the surplus allowance to reclaim from.

returns.ruleset: The ruleset the surplus allowance applies to, as a `JBRuleset` struct.
returns.usedAmount: The amount of terminal tokens used, as a fixed point number with the same amount of decimals as its relative terminal.

DIRECTORY().notice: The directory of terminals and controllers for projects.

PRICES().notice: The contract that exposes price feeds.

RULESETS().notice: The contract storing and managing project rulesets.

accountingContextOf(address,uint256,addr.notice: Returns the accounting context for a terminal's project token.

accountingContextsOf(address,uint256).notice: Returns all accounting contexts for a terminal's project.

balanceOf(address,uint256,address).notice: A project's balance of a specific token within a terminal.

currentReclaimableSurplusOf(uint256,uint.notice: Returns the number of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed from terminals by cashing out a given number of tokens, considering only specific tokens.

Returns the number of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed by cashing out a given project's tokens based on its current ruleset and the given total project token supply and total terminal token surplus.

currentSurplusOf(uint256,address[],addre.notice: Gets the current surplus amount for a project across specified terminals and tokens.

currentTotalReclaimableSurplusOf(uint256.notice: Returns the number of surplus terminal tokens that would be reclaimed by cashing out a given number of tokens across all of a project's terminals using all accounting contexts.

currentTotalSurplusOf(uint256,uint256,ui.notice: Gets the current surplus amount for a specified project across all terminals.

previewCashOutFrom(address,address,uint2.notice: Simulates a cash out without modifying state.

previewPayFrom(address,address,(address,.notice: Simulates a payment without modifying state.

recordAccountingContextOf(uint256,(addre.notice: Registers which tokens a terminal can accept for a project, along with their decimal precision and currency. Called by the terminal when `addAccountingContextsFor` is invoked.

recordAddedBalanceFor(uint256,address,ui.notice: Records funds added to a project's balance.

recordCashOutFor(address,uint256,uint256.notice: Records a cash out — calculates how many terminal tokens a holder receives for burning project tokens.

recordPaymentFrom(address,(address,uint8.notice: Records a payment — calculates how many project tokens to mint based on the payment amount and the current ruleset's weight. Uses the data hook if configured, otherwise mints proportionally.

recordPayoutFor(uint256,address,uint256,.notice: Records a payout during `sendPayoutsOf`, decrementing balance and enforcing the payout limit.

recordTerminalMigration(uint256,address).notice: Records a terminal migration — zeros out the project's balance and returns the amount moved to the new terminal. The current ruleset must allow terminal migration.

recordUsedAllowanceOf(uint256,address,ui.notice: Records a surplus allowance withdrawal — takes funds from the project's surplus (the balance above payout limits) and enforces the surplus allowance cap.

usedPayoutLimitOf(address,uint256,addres.notice: The currency-denominated amount of funds that a project has already paid out from its payout limit during the current ruleset for each terminal, in terms of the payout limit's currency.

usedSurplusAllowanceOf(address,uint256,a.notice: The currency-denominated amounts of funds that a project has used from its surplus allowance during the current ruleset for each terminal, in terms of the surplus allowance's currency.

contractNotice: The accounting engine behind every terminal. Records project balances, enforces payout limits and surplus allowances, calculates how many tokens to mint per payment (based on the ruleset's weight and currency), and determines how much a token holder receives when cashing out (via the bonding curve in `JBCashOuts`).
